title: Weapon Controller Subsystem Static Attributes
description: Static attributes of the weapon controller subsystem, defining fire mode, aim tolerance, input channels and other hardware parameters.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference path, used for editor intelligent suggestions and validation

basic_durability.description: Subsystem basic durability. Becomes paralyzed at 0, restores functionality when repaired to 30%.

pass_damage.description: Whether to pass damage to the subsystem holder. If true, damage received by the subsystem is transferred to the part.

limit_damage.description: Whether to limit damage transfer to remaining durability. e.g. 40 damage, 20 remaining, only 20 transfers.

hidden.description: Whether to hide the subsystem in HUD, commonly used for non-functional parts that only provide damage differentiation.

fire_mode.description: Fire mode: SALVO — all aimed launchers fire simultaneously; RIPPLE — launchers fire sequentially.

ripple_interval_tick.description: Interval between each fire in ripple mode (in ticks). Only applicable when fire_mode is RIPPLE.

aim_tolerance_deg.description: Aim tolerance angle (degrees). A launcher is considered aimed if its direction is within this angle of the target. Set to >180 (e.g. 360) to disable tolerance checking.

aim_inputs.description: List of aim target coordinate input channels. Reads Vec3 world coordinates from any channel. Typically receives ViewInputSignal from CameraSubsystem or SeatSubsystem.

fire_inputs.description: List of fire command input channels. Any non-EmptySignal on any channel is treated as a fire command. Typically receives key-triggered fire signals.

on_destroyed.description: Sound event when the subsystem is destroyed.

on_activate.description: Sound event when the subsystem is activated.

examples.description: Salvo mode example - standard weapon controller configuration
Ripple mode example - multiple target sources and fire command sources
